sections.heading: Syntax
Options
sections.body:
`synonyms` takes the following fields in an index definition:

| Field             | Type     | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Necessity |
| ----------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
| analyzer          | string   | Name of the analyzer to use with this synonym mapping.You can use a synonym mapping to query only fields analyzed with the same analyzer.To use synonyms with stop words, you must either index the field using the Standard Analyzer or add the synonym entry without the stop word.You can use any MongoDB Search analyzer except the following:Language analyzers:lucene.kuromojilucene.cjkCustom analyzer tokenizers and token filters:nGram TokenizeredgeGram TokenizerdaitchMokotoffSoundex Token FilternGram Token FilteredgeGram Token Filtershingle Token FilterwordDelimiterGraph Token Filter | Required  |
| name              | string   | Name of the synonym mapping. Name must be unique in the index definition. Value can't be an empty string.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Required  |
| source            | document | Source collection for synonyms. The source option takes the collection field.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Required  |
| source.collection | string   | Name of the MongoDB collection that is in the same database as the MongoDB Search index. Documents in this collection must be in the format described in the Synonyms Source Collection Documents.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Required  |

sections.heading: Synonyms Source Collection Documents
sections.body:
Each document in the collection specified as the source for the
synonyms describe how one or more words map to one or more synonyms of
those words.

> **Note:** On free tier clusters, the synonyms collection can't exceed 10,000 documents.

sections.heading: Format of Synonyms Source Collection Documents
sections.body:
You must configure each document with the following fields:

| Field       | Type             | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Necessity   |
| ----------- | ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
| input       | array of strings | Required for mappingType: explicit mappings.For explicit mappings, synonyms values are synonyms of each input token. Value can't be an empty or all-whitespace string. You can specify the same input value in multiple documents.                                                                                                                                                        | Conditional |
| mappingType | string           | Type of mapping. Value can be one of the following:equivalent - describes a set of tokens that are equivalent to one another. For an example of this mappingType, see Example.explicit - matches input tokens and replaces them with all alternative synonyms tokens. For an example of this mappingType, see Example.                                                                    | Required    |
| synonyms    | array of strings | Words that are synonyms of one another if mappingType is equivalent or synonyms of input tokens if mappingType is explicit. synonyms must have at least one value.To use synonyms with stop words, you must either add the synonym entry without the stop word or index the field using the Standard Analyzer.For an example of each mappingType see Source Collection Document Examples. | Required    |

The documents in the collection can contain other fields. The
documents in the collection are additive, and mappings are
deduplicated. MongoDB Search synonyms are stored as a separate Atlas
collection, which counts against the same storage quota as any other
collection in Atlas. MongoDB Search might use more compute resources to
apply synonyms from larger synonyms source collections.

> **Warning:** Don't include invalid synonym documents in the synonym source
> collection. MongoDB Search doesn't create indexes if the indexes use synonym
> mappings that reference collections with invalid documents. Only
> include synonym documents that are properly formatted in your
> synonym source collection.
>
> MongoDB doesn't recommend adding synonym documents to synonym source
> collections in a production environment without first validating
> that they are properly formatted and behave as expected in a test
> environment.

sections.heading: Changes to Synonyms Source Collection Documents
sections.body:
If you make changes to your synonyms source collection:

- You don't need to reindex because MongoDB Search watches for changes and
  automatically updates its internal synonym map.

- The time it takes MongoDB Search to update the synonym mappings increases with
  the synonym source collection size. Note that the changes to synonym
  documents are reflected in your MongoDB Search query results eventually.

sections.heading: Source Collection Document Examples
sections.body:
Atlas provides the documents for the following MongoDB Search mapping type
examples in a collection named `sample_synonyms`. You can load these
documents on your cluster in the same database as your collection. To
load these documents on your cluster, when you
[create a MongoDB Search index](ref-create-index) for your collection, do the following:

- When you select the **Configuration Method**, select the
  **Visual Editor**.

- When you **Add synonym mapping** to your index, select
  **Load sample collection** from the **Synonym source
  collection** dropdown.

sections.heading: equivalent mappingType
sections.body:
In the following example source collection document, the `mappingType` is set to `equivalent` so that the tokens
`car`, `vehicle`, and `automobile` are configured to be synonyms of one another.

```json
{
  "mappingType": "equivalent",
  "synonyms": ["car", "vehicle", "automobile"]
}
```

For a [text](text-ref) query for `car`, `vehicle`, or
`automobile` applying a synonym mapping that includes such a
document, MongoDB Search returns documents that contain the term `car`,
`vehicle`, or `automobile`.

sections.heading: explicit mappingType
sections.body:
In the following example source collection document, `mappingType` is set to `explicit`
so that the tokens `beer`, `brew`, and `pint` are configured to be synonyms of
the `input` token `beer`.

```json
{
  "mappingType": "explicit",
  "input": ["beer"],
  "synonyms": ["beer", "brew", "pint"]
}
```

For a [text](text-ref) query for `beer` applying a synonym mapping
that includes such a document, MongoDB Search returns documents that contain
the terms "beer", "brew", or "pint" because the `input` token
`beer` is explicitly mapped to all these `synonyms` tokens.
However, for a query for `pint`, MongoDB Search does not find documents
that contain `beer` because `pint` is not explicitly mapped to
`beer`.

sections.heading: Synonym Source Collection Example
sections.body:
The following collection named `synonymous_terms` is an example synonym
source collection that can be used with the `movies` collection in the
`sample_mflix` database.

The `sample_mflix.synonymous_terms` collection contains the following
documents:

```json
{
  "mappingType": "equivalent",
  "synonyms": ["car", "vehicle", "automobile"]
}
```

```json
{
  "mappingType": "explicit",
  "input": ["race"],
  "synonyms": ["contest", "rally"]
}
```

```json
{
  "mappingType": "equivalent",
  "synonyms": ["dress", "apparel", "attire"]
}
```

```json
{
  "mappingType": "explicit",
  "input": ["boat"],
  "synonyms": ["vessel", "sail"]
}
```

sections.heading: Index Definition Examples
sections.body:
The following examples for the `sample_mflix.movies` collection create
index definitions that use [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings). These examples use the `synonymous_terms`
synonym source collection described in the [Synonym Source Collection Example](sample-synonyms-coll)
section to enable synonym mappings.

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index with a single text field and a single
  synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping configured in
  the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Analyzes the `plot` field with the `lucene.english`
  analyzer.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries
  on fields analyzed with the `lucene.english` analyzer.

You can use the Atlas Administration API to configure the index.
Replace the following values in the sample `curl` command:

- Replace \{`ACCESS-TOKEN`} with your Service Account access token.
  To learn how to generate an access token, see [Make an API (Application Programming Interface) Request](example-api-request).

- Replace \{`groupId`} with the project ID of the project where
  you want to create the MongoDB Search index.

- Replace \{`clusterName`} with the name of the cluster where
  you want to create the MongoDB Search index.

```shell
curl --header "Authorization: Bearer ${ACCESS_TOKEN}" \
    --header "Accept: application/vnd.atlas.2025-03-12+json" \
    --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -X POST "https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/clusters/{clusterName}/search/indexes" \
    -d '{ "collectionName": "movies",
        "database": "sample_mflix",
        "name": "default",
        "type": "search",
        "definition": {
            "mappings": {
              "dynamic": false,
              "fields": {
                "plot": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "analyzer": "lucene.english"
                }
              }
            },
            "synonyms": [
              {
                "analyzer": "lucene.english",
                "name": "my_synonyms",
                "source": {
                  "collection": "synonymous_terms"
                }
              }
            ]
        }
    }'
```

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index for all document fields and a
  single synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping
  configured in the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Uses the default analyzer, `lucene.standard`, to analyze all
  the fields.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries on
  fields analyzed with the `lucene.standard` analyzer.

You can use the Atlas Administration API to configure the index.
Replace the following values in the sample `curl` command:

- Replace \{`ACCESS-TOKEN`} with your Service Account access token.
  To learn how to generate an access token, see [Make an API (Application Programming Interface) Request](example-api-request).

- Replace \{`groupId`} with the project ID of the project where
  you want to create the MongoDB Search index.

- Replace \{`clusterName`} with the name of the cluster where
  you want to create the MongoDB Search index.

```shell
curl --header "Authorization: Bearer ${ACCESS_TOKEN}" \
    --header "Accept: application/vnd.atlas.2025-03-12+json" \
    --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -X POST "https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/clusters/{clusterName}/search/indexes" \
    -d '{ "collectionName": "movies",
        "database": "sample_mflix",
        "name": "default",
        "type": "search",
        "definition": {
            "mappings": {
              "dynamic": true
            },
            "synonyms": [
              {
                "analyzer": "lucene.standard",
                "name": "my_synonyms",
                "source": {
                  "collection": "synonymous_terms"
                }
              }
            ]
        }
    }'
```

To learn how to add a collection that configures words as synonyms to your cluster, and use this collection to
create a MongoDB Search index and run MongoDB Search queries using synonyms, see [How to Use Synonyms with MongoDB Search](synonyms-tutorial).

To see examples of `text` queries that use synonyms, see [Synonyms Examples](text-ref-synonyms-eg) in the [text](text-ref) reference page.

To see an example `phrase` queries that uses synonyms, see [Synonyms Example](fts-phrase-synonyms-example) in the [Phrase](phrase-ref) reference page.

sections.heading: Step 1: Create a  configuration file and specify the  field.
Step 2: Connect to the Atlas CLI
sections.body:
In your terminal, connect to your cluster from the
Atlas CLI. To learn more, see
[Connect from the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli).

sections.heading: Step 3: Create a  index.
sections.body:
Run the [atlas clusters search indexes create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-clusters-search-indexes-create)
command in your terminal, replacing `<clusterName>` with the
name of the cluster, and `<path-to-file>` with the
path to the `static-index.json` file:

```shell
atlas clusters search indexes create <clusterName> --file <path-to-file>
```

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index for all document fields and a
  single synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping
  configured in the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Uses the default analyzer, `lucene.standard`, to analyze all
  the fields.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries on
  fields analyzed with the `lucene.standard` analyzer.

sections.heading: Step 1: Connect to your cluster using .
sections.body:
Open [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass) and connect to your cluster. For
detailed instructions, see [Compass](atlas-connect-via-compass).

sections.heading: Step 2: Specify the database and collection.
sections.body:
On the Database screen, click the `sample_mflix` database, then
click the name of the `movies` collection.

sections.heading: Step 3: Create the  index.
Step 1: Connect to your cluster using .
sections.body:
Open [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass) and connect to your cluster. For
detailed instructions, see [Compass](atlas-connect-via-compass).

sections.heading: Step 2: Specify the database and collection.
sections.body:
On the Database screen, click the `sample_mflix` database, then
click the name of the `movies` collection.

sections.heading: Step 3: Create the  index.
sections.body:
- Click the Indexes tab, then select Search Indexes.

- Click Create Atlas Search Index to open the index creation dialog box.

- Specify a name for the index.

  Your MongoDB Search index is named `default` by default. If you keep this name, then your index will be the
  default Search index for any MongoDB Search query that does not specify a different `index` option in
  its [operators](fts-operators). If you are creating multiple indexes, we recommend
  that you maintain a consistent, descriptive naming convention across your indexes.

- Specify the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) MongoDB Search index definition.

  ```json
  {
    "mappings": {
      "dynamic": true
    },
    "synonyms": [
      {
        "analyzer": "lucene.standard",
        "name": "my_synonyms",
        "source": {
          "collection": "synonymous_terms"
        }
      }
    ]
  }
  ```

- Click Create Search Index.

To learn how to add a collection that configures words as synonyms to your cluster, and use this collection to
create a MongoDB Search index and run MongoDB Search queries using synonyms, see [How to Use Synonyms with MongoDB Search](synonyms-tutorial).

To see examples of `text` queries that use synonyms, see [Synonyms Examples](text-ref-synonyms-eg) in the [text](text-ref) reference page.

To see an example `phrase` queries that uses synonyms, see [Synonyms Example](fts-phrase-synonyms-example) in the [Phrase](phrase-ref) reference page.

The following examples for the `sample_mflix.movies` collection create
index definitions that use [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings). These examples use the `synonymous_terms`
synonym source collection described in the [Synonym Source Collection Example](sample-synonyms-coll)
section to enable synonym mappings.

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index with a single text field and a single
  synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping configured in
  the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Analyzes the `plot` field with the `lucene.english`
  analyzer.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries
  on fields analyzed with the `lucene.english` analyzer.

sections.heading: Step 1: Connect to the deployment by using .
sections.body:
In your terminal, connect to your Atlas cloud-hosted
deployment or local deployment from [mongosh](mongodb-shell). For detailed
instructions on how to connect, see
[Connect to a Deployment](mongodb-shell/connect).

sections.heading: Step 2: Switch to the database that contains the collection for which you want to create the index.
sections.body:
**Input:**

```shell
 use sample_mflix
```

**Output:**

```shell
switched to db sample_mflix
```

sections.heading: Step 3: Run the  method to create the index.
Step 1: Connect to the deployment by using .
sections.body:
In your terminal, connect to your Atlas cloud-hosted
deployment or local deployment from [mongosh](mongodb-shell). For detailed
instructions on how to connect, see
[Connect to a Deployment](mongodb-shell/connect).

sections.heading: Step 2: Switch to the database that contains the collection for which you want to create the index.

```shell
 use sample_mflix
```

**Output:**

```shell
switched to db sample_mflix
```

sections.heading: Step 3: Run the  method to create the index.
sections.body:
**Input:**

```javascript
db.movies.createSearchIndex(
    "default",
    {
    "mappings": { 
        "dynamic": true
    },
    "synonyms": [
        {
            "analyzer": "lucene.standard",
            "name": "my_synonyms",
            "source": {
                "collection": "synonymous_terms"
            }
        }
    ]}
)
```

**Output:**

```
default
```

To learn how to add a collection that configures words as synonyms to your cluster, and use this collection to
create a MongoDB Search index and run MongoDB Search queries using synonyms, see [How to Use Synonyms with MongoDB Search](synonyms-tutorial).

To see examples of `text` queries that use synonyms, see [Synonyms Examples](text-ref-synonyms-eg) in the [text](text-ref) reference page.

To see an example `phrase` queries that uses synonyms, see [Synonyms Example](fts-phrase-synonyms-example) in the [Phrase](phrase-ref) reference page.

The following examples for the `sample_mflix.movies` collection create
index definitions that use [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings). These examples use the `synonymous_terms`
synonym source collection described in the [Synonym Source Collection Example](sample-synonyms-coll)
section to enable synonym mappings.

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index with a single text field and a single
  synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping configured in
  the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Analyzes the `plot` field with the `lucene.english`
  analyzer.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries
  on fields analyzed with the `lucene.english` analyzer.

You can use the Visual Editor or the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) Editor in the Atlas UI
to configure the following index. To configure this index, after you
select your configuration method, select the `movies` collection in
the `sample_mflix` database.

Visual Editor

- Click Refine Your Index.

- In the Field Mappings section, click Add
  Field.

- Click Customized Configuration.

- Configure the following settings in the Add
  Field Mapping window:

  | Field Name   | Enable Dynamic Mapping | Data Type Configuration                                                                                                           |
  | ------------ | ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
  | Select plot. | Toggle to disable.     | Click Add Data Type.Select String from the dropdown.Select lucene.english under lucene.language from the Index Analyzer dropdown. |

- Click Add.

- In the Synonyms Mappings section, click
  Add Synonym Mapping.

- Configure the following settings in the Add Synonym
  Mapping window:

  | Synonym mapping name | Synonym source collection | Analyzer                                                   |
  | -------------------- | ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
  | Enter my\_synonyms   | Select synonymous\_terms. | Select lucene.english under lucene.language from dropdown. |

- Click Add.

JSON Editor

Replace the default index with the following index.

```json
{
  "mappings": {
    "dynamic": false,
    "fields": {
      "plot": {
        "type": "string",
        "analyzer": "lucene.english"
      }
    }
  },
  "synonyms": [
    {
      "analyzer": "lucene.english",
      "name": "my_synonyms",
      "source": {
        "collection": "synonymous_terms"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index for all document fields and a
  single synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping
  configured in the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Uses the default analyzer, `lucene.standard`, to analyze all
  the fields.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries on
  fields analyzed with the `lucene.standard` analyzer.

You can use the Visual Editor or the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) Editor in the Atlas UI
to configure the following index. To configure this index, after you
select your configuration method, select the `movies` collection in
the `sample_mflix` database.

Visual Editor

- Click Refine Your Index.

- In the Synonyms Mappings section, click
  Add Synonym Mapping.

- Configure the following settings in the Add Synonym
  Mapping window:

  | Synonym mapping name | Synonym source collection | Analyzer                                                               |
  | -------------------- | ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
  | Enter my\_synonyms   | Select synonymous\_terms. | Select lucene.standard from the dropdown if it isn't already selected. |

- Click Add.

JSON Editor

```json
{
  "mappings": {
    "dynamic": true
  },
  "synonyms": [
    {
      "analyzer": "lucene.standard",
      "name": "my_synonyms",
      "source": {
        "collection": "synonymous_terms"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

To learn how to add a collection that configures words as synonyms to your cluster, and use this collection to
create a MongoDB Search index and run MongoDB Search queries using synonyms, see [How to Use Synonyms with MongoDB Search](synonyms-tutorial).

To see examples of `text` queries that use synonyms, see [Synonyms Examples](text-ref-synonyms-eg) in the [text](text-ref) reference page.

To see an example `phrase` queries that uses synonyms, see [Synonyms Example](fts-phrase-synonyms-example) in the [Phrase](phrase-ref) reference page.

The following examples for the `sample_mflix.movies` collection create
index definitions that use [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings). These examples use the `synonymous_terms`
synonym source collection described in the [Synonym Source Collection Example](sample-synonyms-coll)
section to enable synonym mappings.

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index with a single text field and a single
  synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping configured in
  the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Analyzes the `plot` field with the `lucene.english`
  analyzer.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries
  on fields analyzed with the `lucene.english` analyzer.

sections.heading: Step 1: Set up your application.
sections.body:
To learn how to install the driver and configure your C application, see the
[Get Started](languages/c/c-driver/current/get-started)
tutorial in the MongoDB C Driver documentation.

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
Step 3: Create the index.
sections.body:
In your terminal, run the following commands to build and run this
application:

**Input:**

```shell
cmake -S. -Bcmake-build
cmake --build cmake-build --target index.out
./cmake-build/index.out
```

**Output:**

```
Index created!
```

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index for all document fields and a
  single synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping
  configured in the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Uses the default analyzer, `lucene.standard`, to analyze all
  the fields.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries on
  fields analyzed with the `lucene.standard` analyzer.

sections.heading: Step 1: Set up your application
sections.body:
To learn how to install the driver and configure your C++ application, see the
[Get Started](languages/cpp/cpp-driver/current/get-started)
tutorial in the MongoDB C++ Driver documentation.

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
Step 3: Create the index.
sections.body:
**Input:**

```shell
c++ --std=c++17 CreateStaticIndex.cpp $(pkg-config --cflags --libs libmongocxx) -o ./app.out
./app.out
```

**Output:**

```
New index name: default
```

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index for all document fields and a
  single synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping
  configured in the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Uses the default analyzer, `lucene.standard`, to analyze all
  the fields.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries on
  fields analyzed with the `lucene.standard` analyzer.

sections.heading: Step 1: Create a  configuration file and specify the  field.
Step 2: Connect to the Atlas CLI
sections.body:
In your terminal, connect to your local deployment from the
Atlas CLI. To learn more, see
[Connect from the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli).

sections.heading: Step 3: Create a  index.
sections.body:
Run the [atlas deployments search indexes create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-deployments-search-indexes-create)
command in your terminal, replacing `<path-to-file>` with the
path to the `static-index.json` file:

```shell
atlas deployments search indexes create --file <path-to-file>
```

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index for all document fields and a
  single synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping
  configured in the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Uses the default analyzer, `lucene.standard`, to analyze all
  the fields.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries on
  fields analyzed with the `lucene.standard` analyzer.

sections.heading: Step 1: Connect to your cluster using .
sections.body:
Open [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass) and connect to your cluster. For
detailed instructions, see [Compass](atlas-connect-via-compass).

sections.heading: Step 2: Specify the database and collection.
sections.body:
On the Database screen, click the `sample_mflix` database, then
click the name of the `movies` collection.

sections.heading: Step 3: Create the  index.
Step 1: Connect to your cluster using .
sections.body:
Open [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass) and connect to your cluster. For
detailed instructions, see [Compass](atlas-connect-via-compass).

sections.heading: Step 2: Specify the database and collection.
sections.body:
On the Database screen, click the `sample_mflix` database, then
click the name of the `movies` collection.

sections.heading: Step 3: Create the  index.
sections.body:
- Click the Indexes tab, then select Search Indexes.

- Click Create Atlas Search Index to open the index creation dialog box.

- Specify a name for the index.

  Your MongoDB Search index is named `default` by default. If you keep this name, then your index will be the
  default Search index for any MongoDB Search query that does not specify a different `index` option in
  its [operators](fts-operators). If you are creating multiple indexes, we recommend
  that you maintain a consistent, descriptive naming convention across your indexes.

- Specify the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) MongoDB Search index definition.

  ```json
  {
    "mappings": {
      "dynamic": true
    },
    "synonyms": [
      {
        "analyzer": "lucene.standard",
        "name": "my_synonyms",
        "source": {
          "collection": "synonymous_terms"
        }
      }
    ]
  }
  ```

- Click Create Search Index.

To learn how to add a collection that configures words as synonyms to your cluster, and use this collection to
create a MongoDB Search index and run MongoDB Search queries using synonyms, see [How to Use Synonyms with MongoDB Search](synonyms-tutorial).

To see examples of `text` queries that use synonyms, see [Synonyms Examples](text-ref-synonyms-eg) in the [text](text-ref) reference page.

To see an example `phrase` queries that uses synonyms, see [Synonyms Example](fts-phrase-synonyms-example) in the [Phrase](phrase-ref) reference page.

The following examples for the `sample_mflix.movies` collection create
index definitions that use [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings). These examples use the `synonymous_terms`
synonym source collection described in the [Synonym Source Collection Example](sample-synonyms-coll)
section to enable synonym mappings.

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index with a single text field and a single
  synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping configured in
  the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Analyzes the `plot` field with the `lucene.english`
  analyzer.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries
  on fields analyzed with the `lucene.english` analyzer.

sections.heading: Step 1: Connect to the deployment by using .
sections.body:
In your terminal, connect to your Atlas cloud-hosted
deployment or local deployment from [mongosh](mongodb-shell). For detailed
instructions on how to connect, see
[Connect to a Deployment](mongodb-shell/connect).

sections.heading: Step 2: Switch to the database that contains the collection for which you want to create the index.
sections.body:
**Input:**

```shell
 use sample_mflix
```

**Output:**

```shell
switched to db sample_mflix
```

sections.heading: Step 3: Run the  method to create the index.
Step 1: Connect to the deployment by using .
sections.body:
In your terminal, connect to your Atlas cloud-hosted
deployment or local deployment from [mongosh](mongodb-shell). For detailed
instructions on how to connect, see
[Connect to a Deployment](mongodb-shell/connect).

sections.heading: Step 2: Switch to the database that contains the collection for which you want to create the index.
sections.body:
**Input:**

```shell
 use sample_mflix
```

**Output:**

```shell
switched to db sample_mflix
```

sections.heading: Step 3: Run the  method to create the index.
sections.body:
**Input:**

```javascript
db.movies.createSearchIndex(
    "default",
    {
    "mappings": { 
        "dynamic": true
    },
    "synonyms": [
        {
            "analyzer": "lucene.standard",
            "name": "my_synonyms",
            "source": {
                "collection": "synonymous_terms"
            }
        }
    ]}
)
```

**Output:**

```
default
```

To learn how to add a collection that configures words as synonyms to your cluster, and use this collection to
create a MongoDB Search index and run MongoDB Search queries using synonyms, see [How to Use Synonyms with MongoDB Search](synonyms-tutorial).

To see an example `phrase` queries that uses synonyms, see [Synonyms Example](fts-phrase-synonyms-example) in the [Phrase](phrase-ref) reference page.

The following examples for the `sample_mflix.movies` collection create
index definitions that use [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings). These examples use the `synonymous_terms`
synonym source collection described in the [Synonym Source Collection Example](sample-synonyms-coll)
section to enable synonym mappings.

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index with a single text field and a single
  synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping configured in
  the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Analyzes the `plot` field with the `lucene.english`
  analyzer.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries
  on fields analyzed with the `lucene.english` analyzer.

[Get Started](go/get-started)
tutorial in the MongoDB Go Driver documentation.

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
Step 3: Create the index.
sections.body:
**Input:**

```shell
go run create_dynamic_index.go
```

**Output:**

```
New index name: default
```

To learn how to add a collection that configures words as synonyms to your cluster, and use this collection to
create a MongoDB Search index and run MongoDB Search queries using synonyms, see [How to Use Synonyms with MongoDB Search](synonyms-tutorial).

To see examples of `text` queries that use synonyms, see [Synonyms Examples](text-ref-synonyms-eg) in the [text](text-ref) reference page.

To see an example `phrase` queries that uses synonyms, see [Synonyms Example](fts-phrase-synonyms-example) in the [Phrase](phrase-ref) reference page.

The following examples for the `sample_mflix.movies` collection create
index definitions that use [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings). These examples use the `synonymous_terms`
synonym source collection described in the [Synonym Source Collection Example](sample-synonyms-coll)
section to enable synonym mappings.

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index with a single text field and a single
  synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping configured in
  the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Analyzes the `plot` field with the `lucene.english`
  analyzer.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries
  on fields analyzed with the `lucene.english` analyzer.

sections.heading: Step 1: Set up your application.
sections.body:
To learn how to install the driver and configure your Java application, see the
[Get Started](java/sync/current/get-started)
tutorial in the MongoDB Java Driver documentation.

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
Step 3: Compile and run the file to create the index.
sections.body:
Compile and run your application in your IDE or your shell.

**Input:**

```shell
javac CreateStaticIndex.java
java CreateStaticIndex
```

**Output:**

```
New index name: default
```

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index for all document fields and a
  single synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping
  configured in the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Uses the default analyzer, `lucene.standard`, to analyze all
  the fields.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries on
  fields analyzed with the `lucene.standard` analyzer.
